_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for all named geographical features in Leonoria
_meta.notes: All names drawn by map.js should ultimately resolve from this file. danger: 1=safe 5=deadly.

regions.name: Eldenmoor
regions.description: Windswept highland moors blanketed in heather and low fog. Ancient standing stones dot the hilltops and unexplained lights wander the bogs at night.
secrets: A druidic circle beneath the oldest stones reportedly predates recorded history

regions.name: The Greywood
regions.description: A vast primordial forest of silver-barked ash and twisted oak. The canopy is so thick that it is twilight even at noon. Fae activity is common near the deeper groves.
secrets: A portal to the Feywild is said to open at the heart of the wood during the solstice

regions.name: Ashfen
regions.description: Vast grey marshes smelling of sulphur and ash. The water runs black in places from ancient volcanic runoff. Home to lizardfolk tribes and will-o'-wisps.
secrets: Submerged ruins of a pre-human civilisation lie under the deepest bogs

regions.name: Stonespine
regions.description: The ragged spine of Leonoria's central mountain range. Dwarven holdfasts cling to the cliffsides and mine tunnels honeycomb the peaks. The air is thin and cold year-round.
secrets: The deepest mine broke through into a vast underground sea three centuries ago; it was sealed immediately

regions.name: Silvermarsh
regions.description: Silver-grassed flatlands threaded with slow rivers and ox-bow lakes. Fertile farming country; the breadbasket of Leonoria. Prone to seasonal flooding.
secrets: Beneath the silver grasses, a network of underground aqueducts built by a forgotten empire still flows

regions.name: Thornwall
regions.description: A tangle of dense briar and thorn-hedge that forms a near-impassable natural wall along Leonoria's eastern frontier. Bandits and exiles make camps in the clearings.
secrets: The thorns were deliberately cultivated by a wizard-king as a border defence; the control word still exists

regions.name: Iceward
regions.description: The frozen north. Permafrost, howling blizzards, and creatures that have adapted to lethal cold. No settlements of note; even experienced rangers avoid lingering.
secrets: A perfectly preserved pre-glacial city is locked inside a glacier, visible but unreachable

cities.name: Valdris
cities.description: The largest city in Leonoria, built at the confluence of the River Mourne and the Greybeck. Ringed by high stone walls and three concentric rings of districts. Trade capital and seat of the High Council.

cities.name: Sorvane
cities.description: A grim fortress-city carved into the moorland cliffs. Heavy walls, narrow streets, and a permanent garrison. Rules the highland passes with an iron fist.

cities.name: Thelindra
cities.description: Built into the giant trees of the Greywood's eastern fringe. Platforms and bridges span the canopy. Elven spires rise above the treetops, catching starlight. Humans are tolerated but watched.

cities.name: Kethara
cities.description: Half carved into the mountain, half built upon a wide ledge overlooking the lower valleys. Famous for its forges, which burn day and night. The air always smells of hot metal and coal smoke.

towns.name: Millhaven
towns.description: A prosperous riverside town built around a cluster of watermills. Flour, timber, and cloth are the main trades. The weekly market draws farmers from all across the Silvermarsh.

towns.name: Duskford
towns.description: Grown around the only reliable ford across the River Mourne in the highlands. Heavily trafficked by caravans. A rough-and-tumble town with more inns than houses.

towns.name: Thornwick
towns.description: Perched on the western edge of the Thornwall. Half of its residents are outlaws, the other half are people pretending they aren't. A good place to find work no-one else will take.
notableFeatures: The Thorn & Flask Tavern (smugglers' meeting point)

towns.name: Greycross
towns.description: A crossroads settlement at the junction of three major roads. A relay station for couriers and a neutral ground for faction negotiations. Controlled by no one, paid tribute by everyone.

towns.name: Ashbridge
towns.description: Built on stilts above the shallower margins of the Ashfen. The only permanent settlement in the fen. Fishermen and alchemist-foragers make up most of the population.
notableFeatures: The Stilt Quarter (all buildings on wooden poles)

towns.name: Westmere
towns.description: A peaceful town on the western shore of Merewater Lake. Fishing, boat-building, and minor trade define its quiet rhythm. Visitors come for the smoked fish and the sunsets.

villages.name: Coldhollow
villages.description: A dozen stone houses huddled against a mountain cliff for windbreak. Goat herders and quarrymen. Outsiders are viewed with quiet suspicion.

villages.name: Briarholm
villages.description: A hidden hamlet behind a curtain of thorn-hedge. The locals know every gap and path through the briar. Not on any official map. Welcomes those who stumble in; rarely lets them leave easily.

villages.name: Fenreach
villages.description: The outermost human settlement on the fen's edge. Three families who have lived here for generations, trading with the lizardfolk and gathering bog-iron.

villages.name: Highcroft
villages.description: Perched on a croft above the fog line. Sheep farming and wool trade. The village elder is rumoured to be a retired adventurer with a basement full of dangerous souvenirs.

villages.name: Root Deep
villages.description: A gnomish and halfling village built into the base of the Greywood's oldest trees. Everything is small, round-doored, and half-buried. Excellent cheese.

ports.name: Saltmere
ports.description: The largest seaport in Leonoria. Built on a natural harbour at the mouth of the River Mourne. A city in itself — half the population has never gone inland.

ports.name: Greyharbour
ports.description: A fortified naval port cut into the moorland cliffs. Grey stone walls rise straight from the sea. Home to Leonoria's naval garrison; commercial vessels are permitted but taxed heavily.

ports.name: The Anchorage
ports.description: No official government, no harbour tax, no questions. Ships anchor in a natural sea-cave system beneath the Thornwall cliffs. Every kind of cargo passes through here.

ports.name: Wavebreak
ports.description: A smaller working port dedicated almost entirely to deep-sea fishing. The docks smell perpetually of fish and salt. Proud, self-sufficient community with no love of outsiders or taxes.

ports.name: Tidehaven
ports.description: A strange port built on an estuary where the Ashfen meets the sea. Accessible only at high tide; at low tide the channel is a mudflat. The locals time everything by the tides.

ruins.name: The Forgotten Keep
ruins.description: A shattered keep on a mountain spur, its towers broken mid-height. Built by a warlord-king a thousand years ago; what destroyed it is unrecorded. The lower halls are occupied by something that does not welcome visitors.
secrets: The vault contains a sealed lich's phylactery that has never been opened

ruins.name: Old Thorngate
ruins.description: The ruins of the original eastern gate of an empire that no longer exists. Massive carved archways still stand, though the walls are long gone. A waypoint for those crossing the Thornwall.
secrets: The carvings, if fully translated, describe a method to control the Thornwall's briar growth

ruins.name: Shadowmere
ruins.description: A village that sank into the Ashfen during an earthquake three centuries ago. The rooftops are just visible above the black water. At night, lights move below the surface.
secrets: The earthquake was not natural; the village was a centre of forbidden magic and was deliberately sunk

ruins.name: Drakenfeld
ruins.description: The scorched remains of a mountain fortress destroyed by a dragon two centuries ago. Melted stone and fused metal everywhere. The dragon moved on, but something worse may have moved in.
secrets: The dragon was summoned deliberately by the fortress's lord, who miscalculated and was the first to burn

ruins.name: Ironwatch
ruins.description: A chain of five watchtowers, now all ruined, that once monitored the moorland border. Each tower is a day's walk from the next. The middle tower is rumoured to still be inhabited.
secrets: The tunnels beneath the towers form a complete underground route across the moor

ruins.name: The Sunken Temple
ruins.description: A temple to a forgotten god, half-consumed by the marsh. Mossy columns still rise from the floodwater and priests of unknown faiths occasionally make pilgrimage here.
secrets: The god is not forgotten — only sleeping — and accepts offerings still

mountain_ranges.name: The Stonespine
mountain_ranges.description: The great central spine of Leonoria, running north to south for three hundred miles. The range forms the backbone of the continent and is riddled with dwarven tunnels.

mountain_ranges.name: The Frosted Crowns
mountain_ranges.description: A jagged northern range permanently lost in blizzard and cloud. No settlements; not even dwarves venture here willingly. The peaks are said to be the petrified bodies of ancient giants.

mountain_ranges.name: The Ember Teeth
mountain_ranges.description: A crescent of low volcanic mountains in the southwest. Two peaks still vent steam; one is believed to be mildly active. The soil around them is exceptionally fertile.

peaks.name: Greymane Summit
peaks.description: The tallest peak in Leonoria. Permanently snow-capped. A single dwarven outpost near its base monitors traffic through the high passes.
secrets: The summit cairn contains a sealed prophecy cylinder placed there in the First Age

peaks.name: Coldfang
peaks.description: A fang-shaped spire of black rock jutting from the glacier. Nothing lives on or near it. Shapes are sometimes seen moving near the top in blizzards.
secrets: A dragon that has not been seen in two centuries is rumoured to sleep at its base

peaks.name: Ashcrown
peaks.description: A squat volcanic peak that exhales a constant plume of white steam. Cultists of a fire deity consider it sacred and make annual pilgrimages to its crater.
secrets: The crater contains a naturally occurring portal to the Elemental Plane of Fire, too small to pass through — for now

peaks.name: Witherhorn
peaks.description: A double-peaked mountain with an uncanny split silhouette. Considered ill-omened by the dwarves. The valley between the two peaks is permanently in shadow.
secrets: An undead lord used this mountain as a throne during the Second Age; its bones are still in the valley

forests.name: The Greywood
forests.description: See region entry. The forest and its region share a name — the trees define the land entirely.

forests.name: Ashwood
forests.description: A tended coppice-forest managed by the city of Valdris for timber. Woodcutters' paths criss-cross it and the edges are carefully maintained. Deeper in, it becomes wilder.

forests.name: The Blackpine Wood
forests.description: A dense forest of black-barked pine that grows right up to the moorland edge. Almost no light reaches the floor. Wolves and worse den here in winter.

forests.name: Willowmere
forests.description: A long ribbon of willow and alder following the banks of the River Mourne south of Valdris. Damp and pleasant in summer; flooded and impassable in spring.

rivers.name: River Mourne
rivers.description: The longest river in Leonoria. Rises in the Stonespine glaciers, crosses the Silvermarsh, and empties into the Grey Sea at Saltmere. The primary highway of trade.

rivers.name: The Silver Thread
rivers.description: A fast, cold, crystal-clear stream that tumbles off the Stonespine through the Eldenmoor. Famous for its silver-trout fishing. Too swift and rocky for boats.

rivers.name: Ashwater
rivers.description: A slow, dark river that winds through the heart of the Greywood. The water has a faint grey colour from ash minerals. Sacred to the wood elves of Thelindra.

rivers.name: Coldwater
rivers.description: A sluggish, near-black river that drains the Ashfen. Smells of sulphur. Wide, shallow, and dotted with mudflats. Not navigable except by flat-bottomed fen-boats.

rivers.name: The Greybeck
rivers.description: A wide moorland river with a persistent grey-brown colour from the peat it carries. Joins the River Mourne at Valdris. Important for the Silvermarsh's irrigation.

rivers.name: River Veld
rivers.description: A braided river that splits into multiple channels across the southern Silvermarsh before reaching the sea near Wavebreak. Rich in eels and waterfowl.

rivers.name: Stonebeck
rivers.description: A ferocious mountain torrent carved deep into the Stonespine rock. Completely unnavigable. Roars year-round. Powers the mills and forges of Kethara.

rivers.name: Silvern Run
rivers.description: A bright, fast stream that runs beneath Sorvane's walls and is channelled through the city as a water supply. Outside the city it runs free through the moor.

rivers.name: The Winding Grey
rivers.description: An unusually serpentine river with exaggerated ox-bow meanders that have become lakes over time. The Grey ox-bow lakes are famous for their mirror-still surface in still weather.

bodies_of_water.name: The Grey Sea
bodies_of_water.description: The cold, slate-grey sea that borders Leonoria to the west and north. Treacherous storms from the north are common. Rich in fish and dangerous sea creatures.

bodies_of_water.name: Merewater Lake
bodies_of_water.description: A large, deep freshwater lake in the southern Silvermarsh. Fed by the River Veld. Still enough in calm weather to perfectly reflect the sky — locals say you can see the future in it.

bodies_of_water.name: The Black Pools
bodies_of_water.description: A series of connected black-water pools in the heart of the Ashfen. The water is opaque and smells of sulphur. Nothing edible lives in them, but strange things live near them.

bodies_of_water.name: The Frosted Mere
bodies_of_water.description: A glacial lake high in the Stonespine. Ice blue and so cold it can kill in minutes. Fed by glacial melt; drains via the Stonebeck.

dungeons.name: The Deep Halls
dungeons.description: An abandoned dwarven mine complex beneath Kethara that broke into something ancient and sealed. The upper levels are storage; the lower levels have not been entered in a century.
secrets: Floor 7 contains the entrance to a pocket dimension that the dwarves accidentally carved into

dungeons.name: The Drowned Library
dungeons.description: Part of the ruins of Shadowmere, partially accessible via diving or magically-dry chambers. An enormous library whose shelves are still intact below the waterline. The books are preserved by unknown magic.
secrets: The warden will assist those who come to read, not steal

dungeons.name: The Thornwall Labyrinth
dungeons.description: A cave system beneath the Thornwall discovered by bandits a decade ago. Now used as a smugglers' route through the briar. The deeper caves have been untouched since the Second Age.
secrets: The deep path exits on the other side of the Thornwall — a secret the smugglers do not know yet

dungeons.name: The Salt Crypts
dungeons.description: Ancient burial crypts beneath the salt flats east of Saltmere. The salt has preserved everything inside perfectly. The dead walk here, though no one knows why.
secrets: The preserved king was entombed alive as a ritual sacrifice and is not pleased about it

landmarks.name: The Moonspire
landmarks.description: The tallest structure in Leonoria, rising above the Greywood canopy. Built from a single piece of starwood and wound with silver. Glows faintly on clear nights. Cannot be entered by non-elves without permission.

landmarks.name: The Four-Stone Marker
landmarks.description: Four massive standing stones at the crossroads of Greycross. They are older than Greycross, older than any road — they were there first and the roads grew toward them. The carvings are in a dead language.

landmarks.name: The Amber Hall
landmarks.description: The seat of Leonoria's High Council in Valdris. Built of golden sandstone and so named for its colour at sunset. The doors have not been forced in recorded history.

landmarks.name: The Singing Glade
landmarks.description: A circular clearing in the Greywood where the wind produces a constant harmonic tone. Bards travel for months to sit here and listen. Wildlife congregates in unusual peace.
secrets: The sound is a communication that no one has decoded — something is speaking very slowly

landmarks.name: Greymane Outpost
landmarks.description: The highest permanently occupied structure in Leonoria, near the base of Greymane Summit. Four dwarves rotate here in month-long shifts. The job is considered a punishment posting.
notableFeatures: The Complaints Ledger (250 years of grumbling)

landmarks.name: The Drowned Arches
landmarks.description: The tops of a series of ancient arched gateways visible above the Grey Sea at low tide, about a mile offshore from Greyharbour. What they guarded is beneath forty feet of water.
secrets: The arches are the upper gate of an ancient city. The full city is intact below.

passes.name: The Kethara Gap
passes.description: The main pass through the Stonespine, guarded by Kethara. Wide enough for wagons. Heavily tolled. The only reliable year-round crossing of the central range.

passes.name: The Lowback
passes.description: A low saddle pass that can be crossed on foot. No road, no toll, no guards — but prone to ambush and closed by snow for four months of the year.

passes.name: The Ember Col
passes.description: A volcanic pass through the Ember Teeth. Warm even in winter due to geothermal venting. The sulphurous air makes prolonged passage uncomfortable.

passes.name: The Irongate Col
passes.description: A narrow, high pass that was once guarded by a fortified gate (long collapsed). Still marked on old maps. Extremely dangerous but the shortest route through the Stonespine.

roads.name: The King's Way
roads.description: The main paved road of Leonoria, running from Saltmere north through Valdris to Sorvane. Maintained by the crown. Wide enough for two wagons abreast. Regularly patrolled.

roads.name: The Moorpath
roads.description: A flagstone road across the Eldenmoor, older than the Kingdom. Subject to flooding and fog. Cairns mark the route where the road becomes invisible in mist.

roads.name: The Southern Reach
roads.description: A well-maintained trade road through the southern Silvermarsh, connecting Greycross to Wavebreak via Westmere. Flat and easy; the most pleasant road in Leonoria.

roads.name: The Deephall Road
roads.description: A dwarven-built mountain road from Kethara down through the Stonespine to the lowlands. Perfectly engineered, carved into cliff faces where needed. Impresses every visitor.

roads.name: The Canopy Walk
roads.description: An elven-maintained route through the Greywood using a combination of ground paths and elevated bridges between trees. Invisible to those who don't know it. Extremely fast for those who do.

roads.name: The Fenpath
roads.description: A series of raised wooden causeways and stepping-stone paths through the Ashfen. Maintained poorly. Some sections are underwater at high water. Local guides recommended.

roads.name: The Thornwall Track
roads.description: Not a real road — more a series of gaps, tunnels through briar, and unmarked trails through the Thornwall. No two travellers will find exactly the same route. Maintained by no one and everyone.

bridges.name: The Stone Ford
bridges.description: Ancient stone stepping-bridge across the River Mourne at Duskford. Not a true bridge — wide flat stones placed in the river. Passable on foot and by horse; impassable to wagons in flood season.

bridges.name: The Mourne Bridge
bridges.description: A grand five-arch stone bridge spanning the River Mourne at Valdris. Wide enough for four wagons side by side. Built 200 years ago and considered an engineering wonder.

bridges.name: Greybeck Crossing
bridges.description: A functional iron-and-stone bridge over the Greybeck where it meets Valdris. Maintained by the Merchants' Guild. Has a toll gate at each end.

bridges.name: The Silver Ford
bridges.description: A narrow plank-bridge over the Silver Thread in the uplands. Wide enough for one person at a time. Replaced every few years as the fast water rots the timbers.

bridges.name: The Ashwater Crossing
bridges.description: An elven bridge of woven living wood that spans the Ashwater. The bridge is still growing. Elven magic keeps it stable and impassable to those of ill intent.

bridges.name: Ashbridge
bridges.description: The great stilt-bridge from which the town of Ashbridge takes its name. Eighty metres of heavy timber spanning the Coldwater. Rattles and groans in strong current.

bridges.name: Stoneback Bridge
bridges.description: A dwarven-engineered stone arch spanning the Stonebeck gorge. The sound of the river below it is deafening. The arch is a single piece of fitted granite — no mortar.

bridges.name: The Portcullis Bridge
bridges.description: A bridge built directly into Sorvane's city wall, with a portcullis at each end. More gate than bridge. The only crossing of the Silvern Run through the city.
